name: know-tool
description: Master the know CLI tool for managing specification graphs. Use when working with spec-graph.json, understanding graph structure, querying entities/references/meta, managing dependencies, or learning graph architecture.
longDescription: A comprehensive skill for working with directed acyclic graph (DAG) representations of software systems. The specification graph uses explicit dependencies between nodes to model both user intent (WHAT chain) and implementation details (HOW chain). This skill teaches dependency rules, entity types, reference categories, meta sections, and all graph operations.
features: Understand specification graph structure (entities, references, meta)
Learn dependency rules and the DAG architecture
Query and explore graph nodes with know commands
Validate graph integrity and detect cycles
Perform gap analysis and build ordering
Reference detailed guides for entity types, references, and workflows
useCases: Querying entities, references, and meta sections

chains.how: project → requirement → interface → feature → action → component → operation
chains.integration: Action connects both chains - what users DO and how the system implements it

nodeTypes.entities: Structural nodes that participate in dependencies

criticalRules: Always validate after modifications with 'know validate'
Respect entity vs reference distinction - entities participate in dependencies, references don't
Follow dependency rules - use 'know rules' to check before adding dependencies
Maintain DAG properties - no cycles allowed, check with 'know cycles'
Use full paths - always use 'type:key' format (e.g., 'feature:real-time-telemetry')
Never add dependencies to entity objects - only in the 'graph' section
Check completeness - use 'know gap-analysis' to ensure full dependency chains

referenceGuides.description: Deep dive on all entity types and their roles
Understanding reference categories and when to use them
Common patterns: adding features, connecting actions, validation

workflow: Understand the type: know rules describe feature
Add the entity: know add feature new-feature '{"name":"...", "description":"..."}'
Check what it can depend on: know rules after feature
Connect dependencies: know add-dep feature:new-feature action:trigger-action
Validate: know validate && know deps feature:new-feature --recursive

installation.note: See project INSTALL.md for setup if know command is not found
